title: crataegus columbiana var. occidentalis
intro: Crataegus columbiana var. occidentalis, also known as Western flowering hawthorn, is a plant species native to the Pacific Northwest region of North America, belonging to the family Rosaceae. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in the treatment of cardiovascular issues due to its high content of flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs), which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Western flowering hawthorn include lowering blood pressure, improving circulation, and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease. Western flowering hawthorn is typically prepared as a tea, tincture, or capsule, and can also be consumed as a raw fruit or berry, although the most effective preparations are often standardized extracts that contain high concentrations of OPCs.
